title: Unit cores and the Soul aspect
display_title: Unit cores and the Soul aspect
wikitext:
[]: Unit cores and the Soul aspect             Quote Post by sindy » Sat Apr 30, 2011 11:21 am One thing I never quite understood in NGE was the interaction between the mother's soul and the unit's core. Already in Episode 1 we are given the line "Reconfigure Unit 01's system for Rei and re-activate!" Again, same line when Kaworu is introduced. One would think that something as vital as the human soul part of the Eva is not so easy to simply "Swap out" for another. Perhaps it is not really the case with Rei and Unit 01, both being somewhat....related to a degree, but Kaworu is meant to use Unit 02 and they were ready to change it for it to do so. (Only to find out there would be no need, since the child could, in theory, set his rate by his own will without changes to the core) So the bottom question here is this: Is the core a unique holder of each soul and by reconfiguring the Eva would it mean the unit's Core to be changed for another? Or is the core like a holder of the Soul, easy to reconfigure to ''swap'' the soul out for another. And if that is the case, where do they keep the replaced soul?

[]: Re: Unit cores and the Soul aspect             Quote Post by katastroff » Sat Apr 30, 2011 11:38 am You pretty much have it right. Yes, scientists in the evaverse can "Swap out" souls. How they do this isn't very clear, but the process of absorbing a soul into an eva is to achieve 400% synchronization with it. Evas require a soul, which is absorbed into their cores, in order for a pilot to operate it. The pilot needs to be genetically related to the soul, to some extent, in order to sync with it properly. Kaworu can manipulate unit 02 because: (1) Kyoko's soul was "shut out" and inactive, so she did not reject him, (2) Kaworu is an angel, and has Adam's soul, so by default, he can sync with the evangelions themselves. Rei and Unit 01 are related, however, that could very well be the reason why Rei can never pilot Unit 01. IMO, Gendo either doesn't want Yui to know or come in contact with Rei for too long because either she'll flip out or something bad might happen. Of course, Rei is known to have piloted Unit 01 before Shinji became the pilot, so I'm not sure.

[]: Re: Unit cores and the Soul aspect             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Apr 30, 2011 12:04 pm katastroff wrote: Of course, Rei is known to have piloted Unit 01 before Shinji became the pilot, so I'm not sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 30, 2011 6:52 pm I think rather than swapping out the soul, they're talking about reconfiguring the MECHANICAL parts of the core unit, i.e, the bit where the entry plug goes in/ what connects it to the rest of the core, or something interface related. Swapping the core would probably be equal to swapping the soul, but they're only "reconfiguring" it. It could be related to the "sort of imprint" the pilot leaves in the EVA - Even post-reconfiguration, Unit 00 and 01 are said to "smell" like their designated owners, and Mari notes that EVA 02 "smells like someone else" One could debate the validity of comparisions because of the different continuities, but removing EVA 02's core in Rebuild was aparently an effective way to "freeze" it. It is put back in, but "reconfigurated" for Mari. (You hear some technobabble in the BG and it clearly says core REintegration. ) Also what's that about "Rei can never pilot unit 01"? She talks like she can get in there at any time and in fact does so in ep 14. It didn't work in ep 19 because Yui wants Shinji to be "the chosen one" (see ep 21) which means he has to come back. She displays the same complete"Do not want" attitude as her son, probably with the intention of getting Gendo to convince Shinji to come back or something.
